title: Lakshya Sen, Tanvi Sharma Lead India at US Open Super 300
indian_angle: Indian fans will follow the progress of top players Lakshya Sen and rising star Tanvi Sharma at this international event.
summary: Lakshya Sen and Tanvi Sharma are set to lead India's challenge at the US Open Super 300, with Sen facing Julien Carraggi and Sharma heading a seven-player women's singles contingent.

title: BAI Announces Selection Tournament for Asia Junior Championships
indian_angle: This tournament is crucial for identifying India's next generation of badminton talent for the prestigious Badminton Asia Junior Championships.
summary: Badminton Association of India has announced a selection tournament to pick players for the upcoming Badminton Asia U17 & U15 Junior Championships 2026.

title: BAI Issues Advisory Against Unauthorized Badminton Premier League
indian_angle: This advisory from BAI protects Indian players and stakeholders from participating in unapproved leagues, ensuring fair play and official sanctioning.
summary: BAI has issued an advisory warning against participation in 'Passion Sphere presents Badminton Premier League – Season 2,' clarifying it is an unauthorized event.

title: All India Senior Ranking Tournament Announced for Chennai
indian_angle: This tournament provides a crucial platform for senior Indian players to compete, earn ranking points, and showcase their talent on the national circuit.
summary: The Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament 2026 will take place in Chennai, Tamil Nadu, from July 16 to 23, 2026.

title: BAI Launches Grassroot Coaches Development Program in Guwahati & Raipur
indian_angle: This program is vital for strengthening the coaching infrastructure at the grassroots level across India, fostering future badminton champions.
summary: BAI has released a circular for a Coaches Development Program at the Grassroot Level, to be held in NCE Guwahati & Raipur from July 15-24, 2026.

title: India to Host BWF World Championships 2026, Mascot Competition Launched
indian_angle: India hosting the BWF World Championships in 2026 is a monumental event for Indian badminton, bringing the world's elite to home soil and inspiring a new generation.
summary: India will host the BWF World Championships in 2026, with the Badminton Association of India inviting expressions of interest for the event, including a mascot competition.
